name: Slow Practice — 30 min technique focus
description: Pick a passage. Find your clean tempo. Nudge up in small steps. Record where you plateau so tomorrow starts there.

items.label: Warm up (5 min)
items.instruction: Loose hands, easy scales, no metronome. Slow practice depends on relaxed muscles — cold hands sabotage the method.

items.label: Pick a passage + target tempo
items.instruction: Choose the passage you want to speed up. Write down the target BPM (the tempo you want to play it AT eventually). This is the finish line — you won't reach it today.

items.label: Find your clean tempo
items.instruction: Start at target - 30%. Play once. Was every note right, every rhythm right, hands relaxed? If yes, you're at clean tempo. If no, drop another 10% and try again. Keep dropping until you can play through with zero errors and zero tension. That's the tempo you'll start from.

items.label: 5 clean at clean tempo
items.instruction: Play the passage 5 times at the clean tempo. Full concentration. If any attempt is not clean, drop the tempo further and restart the count.

items.label: Nudge up in 3-5 BPM steps
items.instruction: Bump the tempo by 3-5 BPM. Play the passage 3 times. Still clean? Bump again. First flub — drop back to the last clean tempo and stay there for 3 more reps. Then try nudging again. Continue until you plateau.

items.label: Alternate slow + target (optional)
items.instruction: Play the passage at your current plateau tempo, then immediately at target tempo, then back to plateau, then target. The contrast is diagnostic — what you can't do at target becomes obvious. Note what specifically is missing.

items.label: Record your plateau
items.instruction: Write down today's plateau BPM. Tomorrow's session starts 3 BPM below this. Over sessions, the plateau creeps up — that's the whole game.
